codeReview.summary: The implementation is well-structured, comprehensive, and fulfills all requirements. The pipeline correctly wires source discovery, AST analysis, mutation, quality gates, difficulty estimation, dataset building, artifact registry registration, and export. The CLI subcommand 'grist-mill tasks generate' is functional with appropriate options. 41 tests cover all expected behaviors including VAL-SYNTH-05 and VAL-TASKFMT-01 assertions. A few minor code quality issues were identified but none are blocking.

issues.description: Dead code: a MutationPipeline instance is created at the top of run() and passed to _process_file(), but _process_file() creates its own MutationPipeline internally. The passed-in 'mutation_pipeline' parameter is never used. This is misleading and wastes resources.
issues.suggestion: Remove the unused MutationPipeline creation in run() and remove the mutation_pipeline parameter from _process_file(). The method already creates its own file_pipeline.

issues.description: Uses __import__('json') instead of a top-level import. The 'json' module is already standard library and should be imported normally at the top of the file for clarity and consistency with the rest of the codebase.
issues.suggestion: Add 'import json' to the top-level imports and replace __import__('json').dumps(...) with json.dumps(...).

issues.description: dataset.add_tasks(filtered_tasks) can raise DuplicateEntry if two mutations produce identical task IDs (extremely unlikely with UUID-based IDs but not caught). No error handling wraps this call.
issues.suggestion: Consider wrapping in a try/except for DuplicateEntry and logging a warning, or pre-deduplicating by task ID.

issues.description: _infer_dependencies includes Python stdlib modules (e.g., 'math', 'typing') in the dependencies list. This is validated by tests but may not be desired for real-world use where dependencies should represent external packages.
issues.suggestion: Consider filtering out stdlib modules or documenting that this is intentional behavior.

issues.description: The --repo option uses exists=False, meaning Click won't validate the path exists before invoking the command. The command then validates manually. This is fine but means users don't get Click's automatic error formatting.
issues.suggestion: This is a reasonable design choice since the command provides richer error messages with rich console formatting. No change needed, just noting for awareness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The synthesis extra (tree-sitter-language-pack) is optional but required for the mutation pipeline and end-to-end task pipeline to function. Workers need to install it manually via 'uv pip install tree-sitter-language-pack' for tests to pass. This is not documented in services.yaml or the library.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: Add to environment.md or services.yaml: 'The synthesis extra requires tree-sitter-language-pack. Install with: uv pip install tree-sitter-language-pack. This is needed for task synthesis features (m4-ast-parser, m4-task-mutation-pipeline, m4-task-pipeline-end-to-end).'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The existing test_all command ('uv run pytest tests/ -m "not integration_local and not integration_provider" -x -q') includes bench/ integration tests that fail with missing openai import. Workers have been running targeted grist-mill test suites instead. The test_all command should either be fixed or a new command should be added for grist-mill-only tests.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: Add a new command to services.yaml: 'test_grist_mill: uv run pytest tests/test_package.py tests/test_schemas.py tests/test_artifact.py tests/test_interfaces.py tests/test_config.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_registry.py tests/test_telemetry.py tests/test_result_parser.py tests/test_dataset.py tests/test_mutation.py tests/test_ast_parser.py tests/test_harness.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_tools.py tests/test_binding.py tests/test_local_runner.py tests/test_environments.py tests/test_task_pipeline.py -x -q'

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Use Python logging module with structured context. No print statements in library code.' The CLI tasks_cmd.py correctly uses rich console for output. However, pipeline.py uses __import__('json') which is an unidiomatic pattern not mentioned in AGENTS.md conventions.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: No change needed to AGENTS.md; this is a minor code quality issue covered in the review above.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker SKILL.md says to run 'uv run pytest tests/ -m "not integration_local and not integration_provider" -x -q' for the full test suite (step 6). This fails due to pre-existing bench/ integration tests. The worker correctly deviated by running a targeted test suite. The skill's verification commands should be updated.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: Update SKILL.md step 6 to note: 'If the full test suite fails on pre-existing bench/ integration tests, run the grist-mill specific test files instead.'

summary: The m4-task-pipeline-end-to-end feature is well-implemented. It wires the full pipeline from source repository discovery through AST analysis, mutation, quality gates, difficulty estimation, dataset building, and artifact registry registration. The CLI subcommand 'grist-mill tasks generate --repo <path>' works correctly. 41 tests cover all expected behaviors including VAL-SYNTH-05 (end-to-end from source repo to task dataset) and VAL-TASKFMT-01 (multi-language task declaration with language, dependencies, and environment requirements). A few minor code quality issues were found (dead code, __import__ usage) but none are blocking. The feature passes review.
